rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel The Depositions Of The Person Most Qualified And Custodian Of Records Of Deft Cahill Construction Services, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, July 24, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the depositions of the person most qualified and custodian of records of defendant Cahill Construction Services, Inc. and production of documents is granted provided that plaintiffs furnish to defendant Mr. Patterson¿s job sites between 1961 and 1975. Defendant is only required to produce information and documents for those job sites of the plaintiff. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied because defendant acted with substantial justification to oppose plaintiffs¿ motion. Defendant¿s opposition was timely filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Objections To Plaintiffs Discovery Requests
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, July 24, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Alta Building Material Co.¿s motion for relief from waiver of objections to plaintiffs¿ discovery requests is granted. Defendant has met the requirements for relief under C.C.P. sections 2030.290, 2031.300 and 2033.280. Defendant, a dissolved corporation, is under no obligation to provide verifications. (Maldonado v. Superior Court (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1390.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
